title: Pink clay melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A gentle floral bar with rose clay, rose geranium, and lavender in a melt-and-pour base
excerpt: Rose clay gives this bar a soft pale-pink colour and mildly drawing action suitable for normal to dry skin. Rose geranium and lavender essential oils add a light floral scent that complements the natural mineral note of the clay. No lye handling required.
sourceNotes: Rose clay usage rate in M&P soap (1-2 tablespoons per 500 g base): cosmetic craft reference and manufacturer guidelines. Rose geranium and lavender EO stability in glycerin soap base: standard cosmetic craft reference. White base preferred over clear for a true pink colour with rose clay.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep rose clay at 2% and essential oils at 2% total of base weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: No cure required. Bars are ready to use once fully firm.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep for one year wrapped or boxed in a cool, dry place. Melt-and-pour bars sweat if left exposed to humid air -- wrap individually in clingfilm.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pale pink cosmetic clay made from a blend of red and white kaolin clays. The pink colour comes from iron oxide. Gently absorbent and suitable for sensitive and dry skin -- milder than bentonite. Used as a natural colourant and mild drawing ingredient in soap and face masks.

content.text: White soap base is the right choice here: clear base with

turns a muted mauve rather than pink, because the translucent base mutes the warm red-pink of the clay. White base gives the most accurate pale pink result. Pre-mix the clay with a little melted base to prevent streaking in the finished bar.

items.prepNote: white opaque base for true pink colour
about 1.4% of base weight; about 140 drops
about 0.6% of base weight; about 60 drops

content.text: Cut the white soap base into cubes. Melt over a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each. Do not overheat. Cool to about 60°C.

Weigh the rose clay into a small bowl. Add one tablespoon of the melted base and stir to a smooth paste -- this dispersion step prevents pink streaks in the finished bar. Stir the paste into the main batch. Add the rose geranium and lavender essential oils and stir gently to avoid introducing bubbles.

Pour steadily into the silicone loaf mould. Mist the surface with isopropyl alcohol to burst any bubbles. Leave to set at room temperature for at least two hours. Unmould when fully firm and slice into 6 to 8 bars with the wire cutter. The colour will be a soft, even blush pink throughout.

Wrap each bar in clingfilm immediately after slicing. Bars are ready to use straight away -- no cure required. The pink colour holds well in the white base and does not fade significantly over the bar's shelf life.
